mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending recession risk, forcing analysts to downgrade earnings estimates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative unemployment signal, or Rank IC stays below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate robust consumer demand expansion, which directly lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint housing and retail sales momentum fails to predict S&P 500 returns above random chance, with Rank IC remaining below 0.02 across all rolling 24-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production contraction reveals supply-side bottlenecks or collapsing aggregate demand, reducing the equity risk premium and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production contractions do not lead to statistically significant declines in 20-day forward returns, or the signal produces positive IC in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth immediately updates corporate revenue trajectories, causing algorithmic and discretionary investors to bid up equity valuations within the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with quarterly GDP momentum, or the signal generates negative IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Policy rates exceeding 4.5% tighten financial conditions sufficiently to suppress equity multiples and dampen capital expenditure, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances when rates sustain above 4.5%, or the 60-day Rank IC fails to drop below -0.05 during the tightening phase.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and stable consumer price indices indicate a non-inflationary labor expansion, supporting earnings growth without triggering aggressive rate hikes, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint payroll and inflation signal produces a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly outperform baseline during soft landing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained housing market contraction precedes broader economic slowdown and reduces consumer spending, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent week when the housing-led downturn regime is active.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns do not decline following sustained housing market contractions, or the signal generates positive IC in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year window.
